records.summary: Drop-in class booking page for Three Boons Yoga's Kreuzberg studio, offering Jivamukti-based classes across two embedded booking tools (Mindbody and Eversports). The weekly schedule with instructor names and times is visible via the Eversports widget, but pricing requires clicking through to a separate page.
records.notes: Schedule is functional but pricing is hidden behind a link, and the page is cluttered with lengthy booking instructions and hygiene rules that bury the core call-to-action; no city/style-specific H1 or LocalBusiness schema evident.

records.summary: Drop-in class booking page for the Mitte studio, mirroring the Kreuzberg page structure with dual Mindbody and Eversports booking widgets and a visible weekly timetable. Prices are not shown on-page but linked to a separate price list.
records.notes: Good class variety and a dense timetable are positives, but the page is bogged down with repeated booking instructions and lacks inline pricing, a clear H1 optimised for search, or structured local SEO markup.

records.summary: Dedicated pricing page listing all membership tiers, class packs, single class rates, and streaming options with exact Euro amounts. Also covers the student/low-income 20% discount scheme and the practice-for-free camera programme.
records.notes: Pricing is comprehensive and clearly itemised (single class €19.98, memberships from €58.95/mo), and the 2-week trial period functions as a first-time deal, but the page has no schedule, mixes streaming and in-studio options without clear separation, and lacks optimised title/H1 tags for search.

records.summary: Drop-in class booking page for the Charlottenburg studio on Kurfürstendamm, with the same dual-widget setup as the other locations and a visible Eversports weekly timetable including Jivamukti, Yin, and specialty formats. Pricing links out to the main prices page.
records.notes: Offers the widest class variety of the three studio pages including beginner-friendly Foundations sessions, but shares the same structural weaknesses: no inline pricing, repeated boilerplate instructions, and weak SEO signals with no location-optimised H1 or schema markup.

records.summary: Booking page for live-streamed Jivamukti yoga classes that run simultaneously with in-studio sessions and are joinable via Zoom from anywhere; a separate Flex-Stream archive is also referenced. Both Mindbody and Eversports widgets are embedded with a dense multi-class daily schedule.
records.notes: The live-stream concept is clearly explained and the timetable is extensive, but prices are not shown on the page, the page title and H1 are not optimised for search queries like 'online yoga Berlin', and the volume of booking-tool instructions creates significant friction.

records.summary: Three Boons Yoga Berlin offers a 180+hour foundational yoga teacher training called 'The Art of Teaching Yoga I,' running over 8 weeks across April–May 2027, with in-person and online options.
records.trainingName: The Art of Teaching Yoga I: Three Boons 180+HR Yoga Teacher Training
records.notes: Dates and hours are clearly stated, but price is absent from the provided content and philosophy-heavy copy appears before key facts, hurting conversion; no teacher bios, schema markup, or reviews are evident.

records.summary: An advanced 153+hour yoga teacher training for existing certified teachers, running May 30–July 12, 2026 in Berlin-Mitte (or online), priced at 1,899 EUR with payment plan options.
records.trainingName: The Art of Teaching Yoga II: Three Boons 153+HR Advanced Yoga Teacher Training
records.notes: Price and dates are present but buried after lengthy prerequisite and philosophy copy; no teacher bios, student reviews, or structured schema are evident, limiting both conversion and SEO performance.

records.summary: A 15+hour Bhakti Yoga mantra and harmonium weekend training at Three Boons Yoga Berlin on February 7–8, 2026, priced at 279–333 EUR, open to all levels with no prerequisites.
records.trainingName: The Art of Bhakti Yoga: Three Boons 15+HR Mantra & Harmonium Training
records.price: 333 EUR (or 279 EUR with own harmonium); 18% discount for certified Jivamukti/3Boons teachers
records.notes: Best-structured page of the set — dates, price, curriculum topics, and a detailed teacher bio are all present; direct booking via Eversports aids conversion, though price and dates still appear mid-page rather than at the very top.

records.pageName: Three Boons 630+HR Yoga Teacher Mentoring Program
records.summary: A long-form 630+hour one-on-one yoga teacher mentoring program at Three Boons Yoga Berlin, spanning 3 months to 2 years, priced at 10 monthly installments of 333 EUR, for teachers already certified within the Jivamukti/Three Boons lineage.
records.trainingName: The Art of Teaching Yoga III: Three Boons 630+HR Yoga Teacher Mentoring Program
records.price: 10 x 333 EUR per month (3,330 EUR total minimum)
records.dates: Rolling / application-based, no fixed dates listed
records.notes: Mentor names are listed but without bios or photos; no fixed dates reduces urgency, and the application-only model makes a direct booking CTA impossible, lowering conversion; SEO is weakened by no schema, no reviews, and a generic page structure.

records.summary: A listing page for Three Boons Yoga retreats offering multiple upcoming Jivamukti Yoga events across Europe in 2026, including locations in Mecklenburg, Rügen, Venice, and Sicily. The page leads with a lengthy philosophical introduction before presenting individual retreat cards.
records.where: Multiple locations: Mecklenburg, Rügen Island, Venice, Sicily
records.notes: This listing page buries all five key facts under a long philosophical intro, mixes four separate retreats without per-retreat pricing, and lacks a daily schedule or clear booking CTA at the top level.

records.summary: Contact page for Three Boons Yoga Berlin featuring a contact form, addresses for two studio locations (Mitte and Kreuzberg), phone numbers, and email addresses. Embedded maps appear present via Google Maps markers, but opening hours are not listed.
records.notes: Key contact details are all present but buried below the contact form rather than above the fold, and opening hours are entirely missing, which hurts usability and local SEO.
